data.title: A whale deposited 7.006 million FARTCO tokens into Coinbase; selling them would result in a loss of approximately $135,000.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, a whale recently deposited 7.006 million FARTCO tokens into Coinbase. These tokens represent a loss of approximately $135,000 if sold, based on what it had accumulated five days prior.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Faces Pressure Amid Macroeconomic Concerns
data.description:
Cryptocurrencies experienced significant pressure during the early Asian trading session on January 26, leading to the liquidation of over $550 million in leveraged long positions, with Bitcoin briefly dipping to $86,000. According to PANews, safe-haven assets like gold and silver continued their upward trend. The heightened risk aversion is attributed to several macroeconomic concerns, including U.S. President Donald Trump's suggestion of imposing a 100% tariff on Canadian imports, the rising risk of a partial U.S. government shutdown as current funding expires on January 30, and uncertainties surrounding potential U.S.-Japan currency market interventions to stabilize the yen. The New York Federal Reserve's "rate check" on the dollar/yen on Friday further heightened market caution regarding yen depreciation.

In response to these macroeconomic uncertainties, the crypto derivatives market has adopted a defensive stance, with an increase in put skew and implied volatility, alongside significant capital flows shifting long-term put positions to lower strike prices. Looking ahead, with a dense macroeconomic event calendar, including tech stock earnings and the Federal Reserve's interest rate decision, implied volatility is expected to remain elevated. Although the Federal Reserve is anticipated to keep rates unchanged, the market will closely watch Chairman Powell's guidance on future policy directions. Until multiple risks, particularly the government shutdown risk, are clarified, cryptocurrency prices may exhibit volatility in the near term.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Potential Four-Month Losing Streak Amid January Options Expiry
data.description: Bitcoin is poised to encounter a rare four-month losing streak, a situation not observed since 2018. According to NS3.AI, this downward trend aligns with the January options expiry, which could influence short-term price fluctuations. Investors are advised to exercise caution, as this combination may heighten market volatility in the coming period.

data.title: Analysis: Bitcoin may face its first four-month consecutive decline since 2018.
data.description: Bitcoin is poised for its fourth consecutive month of decline, a scenario unseen since the six-month losing streak of 2018-2019. Despite having one full trading week remaining in January, Bitcoin has already seen a slight dip, hovering around $87,000. Bitcoin previously recorded monthly declines in October, November, and December, representing significant pullbacks from its all-time high in October, with a drop of approximately 36% from peak to trough during this period. Even during the 2022 bear market, when Bitcoin's price fell from $69,000 to $15,000, it never experienced more than three consecutive months of monthly declines. However, despite the weak spot price, the derivatives market is showing some optimism. Deribit data shows that the notional value of call options with a strike price of $100,000 is the highest, approaching $900 million, indicating that a significant number of traders are betting on a price rebound to six-figure levels. (CoinDesk)

data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet purchased 11,000 ETH from Binance, worth $31.78 million. This whale currently holds 72,000 ETH, worth $208.5 million.

data.title: Mox Bank has been granted an upgraded Type 1 license, becoming the second Hong Kong digital bank to support trading in virtual assets such as BTC and ETH.
data.description: Hong Kong-based digital bank Mox Bank announced that it has received regulatory approval to upgrade its Type 1 license, enabling it to offer virtual asset trading services to clients through integrated accounts. However, at this stage, it only supports USD trading of Bitcoin and Ethereum. Trading fees are divided into four membership tiers, with the lowest tier, Basic, charging 1.25% commission on virtual asset transactions, while the highest tier, Elite, charges 0.5%. Mox Bank is reportedly the second digital bank to receive approval for virtual asset trading. Previously, ZA Bank launched cryptocurrency trading services in July last year, supporting HKD or USD trading of BTC, ETH, SOL, LINK, etc. (Hong Kong Economic Journal)

data.title: Analysts: US Treasuries remain favored despite expectations of Fed rate cuts
data.description: Analysts at UniCredit Investment Institute noted in a report that despite the uncertainty surrounding the US political environment, US Treasury yields have remained relatively stable, reflecting investors' continued preference for US Treasuries. The market widely expects the Federal Reserve to further cut interest rates, which is expected to boost US Treasuries. Furthermore, the resilience shown by the US economy has enhanced its asset attractiveness. While foreign investors may reduce their investment in US assets in the medium to long term, the likelihood of a large-scale sell-off in the short term is low. (Jinshi)

data.title: Riyad Bank's Jeel Collaborates with Ripple for Blockchain Financial Services in Saudi Arabia
data.description: Jeel, the innovation division of Riyad Bank, has announced a partnership with Ripple to advance blockchain-based financial services in Saudi Arabia. According to NS3.AI, the collaboration will focus on cross-border payments, digital asset custody, and tokenization. The initiative includes proof-of-concept projects within Jeel's regulatory sandbox to ensure compliance with blockchain innovation standards. This strategic alliance aims to bolster Saudi Arabia's standing as a regional leader in fintech and digital financial infrastructure.

data.title: French 10-Year Bond Yield Experiences Significant Drop
data.description: French 10-year government bond yields fell by 5 basis points during the day, marking the largest decline since October of last year. According to Odaily, this drop reflects a notable shift in the bond market. The decrease in yield indicates changing investor sentiment and economic conditions in France.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Market Volatility and Investment Strategy
data.description:
On January 26, Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua shared insights on social media regarding recent market fluctuations. According to BlockBeats, Yi noted that the market has experienced significant volatility, with bearish forces reaching their peak. Despite this, he remains confident in the overall bullish trend, supported by the strong performance of U.S. stocks and the country's crypto strategy.

Yi emphasized that their investment strategy remains unchanged, citing a previous investment in Ethereum that yielded a return of nearly 2.5 times. He acknowledged the price correction from $2800 to $2100 due to a Middle Eastern black swan event, but considers such fluctuations within a normal range. Yi humorously remarked on the rollercoaster-like state of being a bull in the current market, where values can swing by millions overnight.

He expressed that greater challenges often lead to higher rewards, and acknowledged that investment is never a guaranteed profit. Yi stated that their ongoing strategy involves accumulating more Ethereum during dips. He also mentioned that recent safe-haven investments have been directed towards gold and silver, but anticipates a moment when profits will be taken and funds will return to the crypto sector. Yi concluded with optimism, asserting that the crypto industry's lowest point will eventually pass.

data.title: Gold Surges Amid Geopolitical Tensions, Bitcoin Struggles as Safe Haven
data.description:
Gold prices have surged sharply, surpassing $5,100 an ounce, driven by geopolitical tensions, policy unpredictability, and increased central bank purchases. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin, in contrast, is lagging as a safe haven, with outflows from ETFs and a volatility premium in options indicating risk rather than protection.

The article highlights four critical changes needed for Bitcoin to attain a status similar to gold as a haven asset. Additionally, it explores three potential scenarios for the evolving relationship between Bitcoin and gold.
